sanskrit: दिवि सूर्यसहस्रस्य भवेद्युगपदुत्थिता । यदि भाः सदृशी सा स्याद्भासस्तस्य महात्मनः ॥
hindiTranslation: अगर आकाशमें एक साथ हजारों सूर्य उदित हो जायँ, तो भी उन सबका प्रकाश मिलकर उस महात्माविराट् रूप परमात्मा के प्रकाशके समान शायद ही हो ॥
englishTranslation: If a thousand suns rose together in the sky, their light would still barely match that great being's radiance.
essence: Even the brightest familiar light cannot measure the cosmic form's radiance.
speaker: Arjuna
simpleMeaning: Arjuna says that even a thousand suns rising at once would not match the radiance of Krishna's cosmic form.
simpleInsight: Some encounters are so overwhelming that ordinary language and ordinary sight both fail.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, Arjuna is staring into Krishna's cosmic form after the battlefield has frozen into awe. He tries to describe the impossible: even a thousand suns would not equal that radiance. The next verse shows him seeing the whole universe inside that body.
modernRelevance: You stand in a dark room and suddenly the screen flashes open with a truth you cannot unsee. The mind reaches for comparison, but the experience already exceeds it.
translationLiteral: दिवि (in the sky) / सूर्यसहस्रस्य (of a thousand suns) / भवेत् (would be) / युगपत् (at once) / उत्थिता (risen) / यदि (if) / भाः (light) / सदृशी (equal) / सा (that) / स्यात् (would be) / भासः (radiance) / तस्य (of that) / महात्मनः (of the great being)
insight: It shifts the mind from measuring the divine by familiar things to admitting that ordinary scales collapse before it.
